title: CatBoost Ordered-Categorical Specialist For Driver-Race Context
summary: Your current best solutions rely on hand-built frequency features, but CatBoost’s ordered categorical statistics are designed to reduce prediction shift and usually outperform naive frequency/mean encodings on high-cardinality categories like Driver, Race, and Driver x Compound.
rationale: Your current best solutions rely on hand-built frequency features, but CatBoost’s ordered categorical statistics are designed to reduce prediction shift and usually outperform naive frequency/mean encodings on high-cardinality categories like Driver, Race, and Driver x Compound.
implementation_hint: Train a separate CatBoost specialist on mostly raw columns plus a small set of numeric strategy features. Let CatBoost ingest Driver, Race, Compound, Year, and cross features such as Driver x Compound and Race x Compound as categorical inputs. Blend its out-of-fold predictions with the current best tabular model rather than replacing the stack outright.
expected_effect: Improves generalization on sparse drivers/races and captures non-linear categorical interactions without manual leakage-prone encodings.
risk: If folds are not grouped by race-year, CatBoost can still overfit race identity; standalone performance may trail the ensemble if numeric engineering is underpowered.
